sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: F5 (FFIV) Valuation Check As Recurring Software Revenue And Margins Shape The Story
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) shows recent share price strength despite a 1-day decline, with momentum built over the past three years. Its valuation narrative assesses it as "about right" at $310.30, close to its current trading price, driven by a shift to high-margin recurring software revenue. However, questions remain regarding slower software uptake and competitive pressures from large cloud providers, while its P/E ratio appears cheaper than peers but slightly above its fair ratio.

feed.title: O'Reilly Auto Parts to open its 52nd CT store at the Milford Plaza
feed.summary: O'Reilly Auto Parts, a Missouri-based retailer, plans to open its 52nd Connecticut store in the Milford Plaza later this year. The new location will occupy a 36,000 square foot space previously held by Bob's Stores. This expansion makes O'Reilly Auto Parts the fourth-largest auto parts retailer in Connecticut, behind Advance Auto, NAPA, and Auto Zone.

feed.title: Global IT Spending To Reach $6.31 Trillion in 2026, Gartner Projects
feed.summary: Gartner projects global IT spending to hit $6.31 trillion in 2026, marking a 13.5% increase from 2025, primarily driven by investments in AI infrastructure. Data center systems are expected to see the highest growth at 55.8%, with IT services leading overall spending. The forecast highlights the significant impact of AI workloads and high-performance computing on the growing IT market.

feed.title: ChartWatch ASX Scans: CSL, Cochlear, Resmed, IDP Education, Harvey Norman, Nick Scali, Judo Capital, Global X Hydrogen ETF

Editor Carl Capolingua highlights stocks like Altair Minerals and Global X Hydrogen ETF in uptrends, while CSL, Cochlear, and Resmed are noted in significant downtrends. feed.title: DTE Energy (DTE) to Release Quarterly Earnings on Thursday
feed.summary: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) is scheduled to release its Q1 2026 earnings before the market opens on Thursday, April 30th. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $2.03 per share on $4.5345 billion in revenue. The company has a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average target price of $155.23, although a recent permit denial for a solar park introduces potential risk for its renewables portfolio.

feed.title: Quanta Services (PWR) Expected to Announce Earnings on Thursday

Shares are trading near their 1-year high, and the company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $599.

feed.title: The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) Price Target Reduced to $167 by BofA Amid Higher Resin Cost Expectations
feed.summary: BofA Securities has lowered its price target for The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) from $171 to $167, citing expectations of higher resin costs, while maintaining a Buy rating. Despite this, P&G's Board of Directors approved a 3% increase in its quarterly dividend to $1.0885 per share, payable on or after May 15, 2026. The company is considered one of the most undervalued Dow stocks, known for its global consumer goods brands and commitment to consistent dividend growth.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (MRK) Gains FDA Priority Review for KEYTRUDA Regimen in Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (MRK) has received FDA Priority Review for its KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX regimen when used in combination with Padcev for muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients eligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy. The FDA set an August 17, 2026 target action date, with the applications supported by positive Phase 3 KEYNOTE-B15 data showing improved outcomes and survival benefits. This news follows Merck's recent announcement of a tender offer to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals for $53.00 per share, expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Guggenheim Adjusts Amgen Inc. (AMGN) PT to $351, Cites Updated Model Ahead of Earnings
feed.summary: Guggenheim has increased its price target for Amgen Inc. (AMGN) to $351 from $347, maintaining a Neutral rating, as it updates its financial model before the company's first-quarter earnings release on April 30. Separately, Amgen announced positive Phase 3 results for TEPEZZA's subcutaneous delivery in thyroid eye disease, demonstrating comparable efficacy and improved convenience over the intravenous form. The article also notes Amgen's position as a leading biotechnology company, though it suggests other AI stocks might offer greater return potential.

feed.title: Uber riders complain they were charged more for paying with Amex cards—one viral video shows a $13 difference
feed.summary: Uber riders are increasingly reporting higher fares when using American Express cards or other Uber-related perks like Uber Cash or Uber One, leading to concerns about opaque dynamic pricing algorithms. A viral video demonstrated a $13 difference for the same ride when switching from an Amex to a Visa card. While Uber denies that payment method affects pricing, users suggest the alleged price increases may undermine the benefits offered by Amex and other programs.

feed.title: Jacobs lands 8-year role on U.K. projects tied to a low-carbon grid
feed.summary: Jacobs (NYSE: J) has secured an eight-year multi-disciplinary framework agreement with Murphy to support projects within its SSEN Transmission portfolio in the U.K. This collaboration will focus on transmission and distribution infrastructure development to advance the U.K.'s low-carbon future. Jacobs will provide integrated engineering, environmental, and civil services, building on previous successful projects like the Uxbridge Moor substation.
